title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 49.
content:
There was an Old Man or Nepaul,
    From his horse had a terrible fall;
    But, though split quite in two,
    By some very strong glue,
    They mended that Man of Nepaul.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 49.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
